title: malus malus
intro:
Malus malus is not a recognized botanical name. However, the Malus genus includes the common apple tree (Malus domestica), and one possible answer could be Malus baccata, also known as the Siberian crabapple. 

Malus baccata, also known as the Siberian crabapple, is a deciduous shrub or small tree native to Siberia and parts of Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of Malus baccata include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and support the immune system. Malus baccata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
